companion_files.content:
# ARCHIVE_NOTE.md

- Archived on: 2026-02-14
- Archived from former umbrella-context execution track (historical parent now at `plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-umbrella/`)
- Reason: Bundle F metadata/library polish execution slice is functionally complete; deferred scope was extracted to dedicated long-term review tracks.
- Canonical archived plan:
  - `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-bundle-f-metadata-library-polish/PLAN.md`

companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# BUNDLE_F_METADATA_LIBRARY_POLISH_EXECUTION_PLAN.md

## Document Status

- Last updated: 2026-02-14
- Feature: Bundle F metadata/library polish execution slice
- Parent feature: `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-umbrella/PLAN.md`
- Scope: Archived execution record (implementation completed)
- Planning maturity: `Spec Outline` complete, `Refined Spec` complete, `Detailed Implementation Plan` complete
- Implementation state: Complete and archived (`2026-02-14`)

## Goal

Deliver the remaining high-value metadata/library polish work from Bundle F with low regression risk by prioritizing:
1. string-normalization parity where it improves matching reliability,
2. image fetch/cache hardening parity and validation,
3. regression coverage for metadata/download/library flows,
while keeping lower-priority MBID UI and Lidarr enhancements gated behind explicit scope checks.

## Scope Decisions (This Slice)

1. In scope now:
   - Task `5.1` normalization helper parity (adapt-only).
   - Task `5.4` image proxy/cache reliability parity (adapt-only).
   - Task `5.6` regression test expansion and verification.
2. Gated/deferred unless user promotes:
   - Task `5.3` MBID/manual release-correction UI (`MusicBrainzLookup`, `ReleaseSelectionModal` patterns).
   - Task `5.5` Lidarr quality-profile and queue-safety deltas.
   - Extracted as standalone long-term feature tracks:
     - `plans/current/mbid-release-correction-long-term-review/MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md`
     - `.agents/plans/current/lidarr-quality-delete-safety-long-term-review/PLAN.md`
3. Preserve already shipped Bundle F work:
   - Task `5.2` playlist diversity is complete and out of this execution slice.

## Known-Gap Ledger Review (Bundle F)

Reviewed on 2026-02-14 before drafting this slice:
1. MBID search UI/API parity is functionally useful but not a blocker for existing metadata override workflows.
2. Lidarr quality-profile configurability and delete queue-safety are reliability improvements, not hard blockers for current baseline behavior.
3. Decision for this slice: keep `5.3` and `5.5` gated; concentrate active execution on `5.1`, `5.4`, `5.6`.
4. Post-closeout decision: keep `5.3` and `5.5` outside Bundle F and execute them only through their dedicated long-term feature tracks.

## Full-Stack Request Path Baseline

For HTTP/API-facing sub-work in this slice, validate both backend-direct and frontend-base paths:
1. Client URL/base path:
   - Frontend runtime: `/api/*` through Next.js app (`frontend`).
2. Frontend proxy behavior:
   - `frontend/lib/api.ts` calls backend via frontend proxy/base URL.
3. Backend path:
   - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/releases.ts`
4. Downstream dependencies:
   - PostgreSQL via Prisma (`backend/src/utils/db.ts`)
   - Redis cache (`cover-art`/metadata caching paths)
   - External APIs (MusicBrainz, Deezer, Fanart, Cover Art Archive, Lidarr)
5. Deployment modes to evaluate in validation tasks:
   - `docker-compose.aio.yml`
   - `docker-compose.yml` (split stack)
   - local host-run (`docker-compose.local.yml` + host frontend/backend)

## Spec Outline

1. Normalize upstream lookup inputs consistently where this improves external match quality without broad behavior churn.
2. Harden image proxy/cache path parity and ensure cache lifecycle is bounded.
3. Validate metadata editing, cover-art, and download/release flows against regressions.
4. Keep MBID UI and Lidarr profile deltas ready but gated until explicit promotion.
5. Sync docs only for behavior that is actually shipped.

## Refined Spec

### Constraints

1. No broad refactor of metadata stack; adapt-only changes in targeted files.
2. Keep current API contracts stable unless parity endpoints are explicitly added.
3. Preserve existing cover-art and metadata fallback behavior.
4. Any new settings/schema additions must be migration-safe and explicitly justified.
5. All implementation tasks require route/service tests plus one full-stack verification checkpoint.

### Target Touchpoints (Primary)

1. Normalization:
   - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`
   - `backend/src/utils/artistNormalization.ts`
2. Image/cache reliability:
   - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
   - `backend/src/services/fanart.ts` (if touched by integration)
   - `frontend/public/sw.js`
   - `frontend/components/ui/CachedImage.tsx`
3. Regression tests:
   - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/*`
   - `backend/src/tests/*`
   - `frontend/tests/e2e/predeploy/*`
4. Gated MBID/release UX:
   - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
   - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
5. Gated Lidarr reliability/settings:
   - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
   - `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`
   - `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`

### Acceptance Criteria (This Slice)

1. Normalization parity deltas are selective, tested, and do not regress existing lookups.
2. Image fetch/cache behavior is measurably more robust (cache hit behavior, bounded misses, no breakage of existing cover paths).
3. Metadata/download/release regression tests are expanded and pass.
4. Gated items (`5.3`, `5.5`) have explicit go/no-go checkpoints with required prerequisites documented.

## Detailed Implementation Plan (Atomic Tasks)

### Status Legend

- `Pending`: Not started
- `In Progress`: Actively being worked
- `Complete`: Finished and includes completion summary

Completion summary template (required when task is `Complete`):

- Delivered:
- Files/functions/hooks touched:
- Verification/testing:
- Plain-English impact:

## Phase 0: Re-Context + Contract Freeze

### Task 0.1: Freeze Bundle F execution scope for this slice
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit in-scope/out-of-scope list (`5.1`, `5.4`, `5.6` in; `5.3`, `5.5` gated).
- References:
  - `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-umbrella/PLAN.md`
  - `.agents/plans/archived/fjordnode-feature-port-bundle-f-metadata-library-polish/PLAN.md`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Scope frozen to `Task 5.1`, `Task 5.4`, `Task 5.6`; gated/deferred `Task 5.3` and `Task 5.5`.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched: planning-only update in this execution plan.
  - Verification/testing: Cross-checked with active handoff state and user directive to begin Bundle F execution.
  - Plain-English impact: Work is now explicitly limited to low-risk metadata/image/test polish, with larger MBID/Lidarr deltas held for explicit promotion.

### Task 0.2: Capture baseline full-stack probe matrix
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Command list and expected responses for backend-direct and frontend-base probes for cover-art + metadata paths.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Captured executable probe matrix and recorded real command/output evidence for both failure and success runtime states of backend-direct and frontend-base paths.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched: planning-only updates in this execution plan; runtime probe commands used `backend/src/index.ts` health endpoints and frontend proxy paths.
  - Verification/testing:
    - Failure-mode baseline captured before local infra bootstrap:
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:3007/api/health` -> connection refused.
      - `curl -i --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/health` -> `503` `UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE`.
    - Success-mode verification captured after dependency bootstrap:
      - `docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d postgres redis` (pass)
      - `cd backend && DATABASE_URL=postgresql://lidify:lidify@127.0.0.1:5433/lidify npx prisma migrate deploy` (pass)
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:3007/api/health` -> `200` `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/health` -> `200` `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`
  - Plain-English impact: Operators now have a verified local runbook that shows both what failure looks like (infra down) and what healthy backend/proxy responses look like after stack bootstrap.

## Phase 1: Task 5.1 Normalization Parity (Medium Priority)

### Task 1.1: Map current normalization logic vs fjord helper utility
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Diff ledger identifying where `normalizeQuotes` / fullwidth normalization materially improves lookups.
- References:
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/utils/stringNormalization.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Diff mapping confirmed high-value parity points at external lookup boundaries: `imageProvider` Deezer/Fanart/CoverArt requests, `musicbrainz` search normalization input, and duplicated quote/fullwidth handling in `spotifyImport`.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched: analysis across `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`, `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`, `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`, and fjord utility `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/utils/stringNormalization.ts`.
  - Verification/testing: Compared current vs fjord service diffs and validated adapter approach against existing rate-limiter/service contracts before coding.
  - Plain-English impact: We identified exactly where weird quote/fullwidth text can break metadata lookups and targeted only those spots.

### Task 1.2: Implement selective normalization adaptations
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Targeted normalization usage in lookup paths with minimal behavior churn.
- References:
  - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added shared normalization utility (`normalizeQuotes`, `normalizeFullwidth`) and applied it selectively to lookup-heavy paths; wrapped image-provider external calls in global limiter channels where parity and safety warranted.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/utils/stringNormalization.ts`
    - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts` (`normalizeLookupValue`, normalized Deezer matching/querying, Fanart/CoverArt limiter wrapping)
    - `backend/src/services/musicbrainz.ts` (`normalizeForSearch` input normalization)
    - `backend/src/services/spotifyImport.ts` (`normalizeString` / `normalizeApostrophes` use shared helper)
    - `backend/src/services/rateLimiter.ts` (added typed/configured `fanart` limiter profile)
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
  - Plain-English impact: Metadata lookups are now less likely to fail on curly quotes or fullwidth/stylized text, without changing overall workflow behavior.

### Task 1.3: Add/expand normalization tests
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Unit coverage for curly-quote/fullwidth and lookup query normalization edge cases.
- References:
  - `backend/src/tests/artistNormalization.test.ts`
  - new/updated tests under `backend/src/services/__tests__/` as needed
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added focused utility tests for quote/fullwidth normalization edge cases.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/utils/__tests__/stringNormalization.test.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npx jest src/utils/__tests__/stringNormalization.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm test -- --runInBand` (attempted; test suite produced known Redis reconnect logging noise and did not provide a clean completion signal in this environment)
  - Plain-English impact: We now have automated checks that protect the new text-normalization behavior from regressions.

## Phase 2: Task 5.4 Image Proxy/Cache Reliability (Medium Priority)

### Task 2.1: Security and cache-path gap audit for cover-art route
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit parity decisions for URL safety checks, redirect handling, negative caching, and cache cleanup behavior.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/imageProxy.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/imageCacheCleanup.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Completed parity audit and selected adapt-only hardening decisions for cover-art/color proxying.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - Audit references:
      - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
      - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/imageProxy.ts`
      - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/imageCacheCleanup.ts`
  - Verification/testing: Reviewed full current/fjord cover-art and color-fetch paths, including cache keying and fetch error handling behavior.
  - Plain-English impact: The image proxy work now has a clear, low-risk hardening plan focused on blocking unsafe URLs and handling redirects safely.
  - Explicit parity decisions:
    - URL safety checks: `Adopt` (block private/internal targets for external image proxying).
    - Redirect handling: `Adopt` (manual, bounded redirects with per-hop URL validation).
    - Negative caching: `Keep existing` Redis 404 caching behavior for `/library/cover-art`.
    - Cache cleanup: `Defer` fjord external-disk-cache cleanup path in this slice (no new external disk cache layer introduced here; existing Redis TTL caching remains bounded).

### Task 2.2: Implement selected backend cache/proxy hardening deltas
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Adapted hardening changes with preserved existing route contracts.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/imageStorage.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/imageProvider.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added hardened external image proxy service and integrated it into cover-art and color-extraction routes without changing endpoint contracts.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/services/imageProxy.ts` (URL validation, safe redirect traversal, retries, typed fetch result)
    - `backend/src/routes/library.ts` (`/cover-art` and `/cover-art-colors` now use hardened proxy path)
    - `backend/src/services/__tests__/imageProxy.test.ts` (unit coverage for URL validation + fetch behavior)
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npx jest src/services/__tests__/imageProxy.test.ts src/utils/__tests__/stringNormalization.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
  - Plain-English impact: Cover art and palette extraction now reject unsafe/private URLs and handle redirects more safely, reducing SSRF-style risk while preserving existing client behavior.

### Task 2.3: Reconcile frontend image caching strategy
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Decision + implementation (if needed) for `CachedImage`/service-worker behavior parity without regressing current UX/perf.
- References:
  - `frontend/components/ui/CachedImage.tsx`
  - `frontend/public/sw.js`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/CachedImage.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/hooks/useCachedImage.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/utils/imageCache.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Frontend cache-strategy reconciliation completed with explicit `Keep current implementation` decision (no code changes required).
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - Reviewed current:
      - `frontend/public/sw.js`
      - `frontend/components/ui/CachedImage.tsx`
    - Reviewed fjord parity references:
      - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/CachedImage.tsx`
      - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/hooks/useCachedImage.ts`
      - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/utils/imageCache.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - Full-file side-by-side behavior review: current fork already provides service-worker cache-first image handling plus request throttling and cache trimming; fjord uses client blob-url cache with lower cross-tab reuse and higher in-memory object-URL churn risk.
  - Plain-English impact: We keep the stronger existing frontend image caching model and avoid adding extra client-side complexity that could regress performance or memory usage.

### Task 2.4: Add backend route tests for cover-art cache/error paths
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Route-level coverage for cache hit/miss, 404 caching, error fallback, and header behavior.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/library.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/` (new/expanded)
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added route-level compatibility tests for hardened cover-art and color-proxy behavior (invalid URL blocking, cached 404 handling, 404 negative-caching write-through, and fetch-error response mapping).
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/libraryCoverArtCompat.test.ts`
    - `backend/src/routes/library.ts` (covered handlers)
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npx jest src/routes/__tests__/libraryCoverArtCompat.test.ts src/services/__tests__/imageProxy.test.ts src/utils/__tests__/stringNormalization.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
  - Plain-English impact: We now have automated guardrails around the most failure-prone cover-art proxy paths, reducing regression risk as metadata/image work continues.

## Phase 3: Task 5.6 Regression Tests + Full-Stack Verification

### Task 3.1: Expand metadata/editing regression coverage
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Route tests covering artist/album/track metadata update/reset flows and API path correctness.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
  - `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added metadata override/reset compatibility tests for enrichment endpoints and fixed frontend track metadata API path mismatch.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts`
    - `frontend/lib/api.ts` (`updateTrackMetadata` now targets `/enrichment/tracks/:id/metadata`)
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npx jest src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - Included in expanded Bundle F regression run: `cd backend && npx jest src/routes/__tests__/libraryCoverArtCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts src/routes/__tests__/enrichmentMetadataCompat.test.ts src/services/__tests__/imageProxy.test.ts src/utils/__tests__/stringNormalization.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
  - Plain-English impact: Manual metadata edits and resets now have automated guardrails, and the frontend is now calling the correct backend endpoint for track metadata updates.

### Task 3.2: Expand download/release regression coverage relevant to Bundle F
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Tests for metadata-dependent download/release paths and non-regression checks.
- References:
  - `backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/releases.ts`
  - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Added route compatibility tests for download-history filtering and release-radar status/downloadability classification.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - `backend/src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts`
  - Verification/testing:
    - `cd backend && npx jest src/routes/__tests__/downloadsReleasesCompat.test.ts --runInBand` (pass)
    - Included in expanded Bundle F regression run (same command as Task `3.1`) (pass)
    - `cd backend && npm run build` (pass)
  - Plain-English impact: Discovery downloads remain correctly hidden unless explicitly requested, and release radar behavior stays predictable for upcoming vs downloadable releases.

### Task 3.3: Full-stack verification checkpoint (required)
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Recorded command/output evidence for:
  - backend-direct probes,
  - frontend-base probes,
  - split/local mode validation (where applicable).
- References:
  - `docker-compose.yml`
  - `docker-compose.aio.yml`
  - `docker-compose.local.yml`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Executed backend-direct and frontend-base probe commands, first in failure mode (infra absent) and then with successful live responses after stack bootstrap.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched: no code changes for this task; runtime checks used host-run startup and curl probes.
  - Verification/testing:
    - Infra bootstrap and schema readiness:
      - `docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d postgres redis` (pass)
      - `cd backend && DATABASE_URL=postgresql://lidify:lidify@127.0.0.1:5433/lidify npx prisma migrate deploy` (pass)
    - Backend host-run:
      - `cd backend && PORT=3007 DATABASE_URL=postgresql://lidify:lidify@127.0.0.1:5433/lidify REDIS_URL=redis://127.0.0.1:6380 ENCRYPTION_KEY=... SESSION_SECRET=... MUSIC_PATH=/tmp npm run dev` (startup pass)
    - Frontend host-run:
      - `cd frontend && PORT=3031 BACKEND_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3007 npm run dev` (startup pass)
    - backend-direct probes:
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:3007/api/health` -> `200`
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:3007/api/enrichment/tracks/test-track/metadata ...` -> `401 Not authenticated` (expected without token, confirms route mount)
    - frontend-base probes:
      - `curl -i --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/health` -> `200` (proxy path healthy)
      - `curl -i --max-time 10 -X PUT http://127.0.0.1:3031/api/enrichment/tracks/test-track/metadata ...` -> `401 Not authenticated` (expected without token, confirms proxy + route path correctness)
  - Plain-English impact: End-to-end request flow is now live-verified for this slice; both direct backend URLs and frontend proxy URLs resolve correctly with expected authentication behavior.

## Phase 4: Gated Follow-Ups (`5.3` and `5.5`)

### Task 4.1: Gate review for MBID/release-selection UI scope promotion
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit yes/no decision with prerequisites and blast-radius notes.
- References:
  - `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/MusicBrainzLookup.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/ReleaseSelectionModal.tsx`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Decision = `No-Go (defer)` for this Bundle F slice.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - Reviewed current: `frontend/components/MetadataEditor.tsx`
    - Reviewed fjord parity UI: `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/MusicBrainzLookup.tsx`, `../lidify-fjordnode/frontend/components/ui/ReleaseSelectionModal.tsx`
    - Reviewed fjord API dependencies: `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/enrichment.ts`, `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/downloads.ts`
    - Verified current fork does not expose required parity endpoints (`/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/artists`, `/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/release-groups`, `/downloads/releases/:albumMbid`, `/downloads/grab`)
  - Verification/testing: Full-file parity audit + route-surface grep review completed; existing metadata override/reset regression coverage remains green for current path contracts.
  - Plain-English impact: Current manual metadata editing remains stable and tested; interactive MBID search/release-picking UI is deferred because it would require coordinated frontend + new backend endpoint work with wider download-flow blast radius. The deferred work is now captured in `plans/current/mbid-release-correction-long-term-review/MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md`.

### Task 4.2: Gate review for Lidarr quality-profile and delete-safety promotion
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Explicit yes/no decision on adding `lidarrQualityProfileId` setting and queue-aware artist-delete safeguards.
- References:
  - `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
  - `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`
  - `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`
  - `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Decision = `No-Go (defer)` for this Bundle F slice.
  - Files/functions/hooks touched:
    - Reviewed current: `backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`, `backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`, `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
    - Reviewed fjord parity: `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/services/lidarr.ts`, `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/src/routes/systemSettings.ts`, `../lidify-fjordnode/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`
  - Verification/testing:
    - Confirmed fjord-only persisted setting path for `lidarrQualityProfileId` (schema + settings route + service reads) is absent in current fork.
    - Confirmed current delete paths (`deleteArtist`, `deleteArtistById`) do not embed fjord's `hasActiveDownloads` guard, while current fork already has newer queue snapshot/reconciliation utilities.
    - Confirmed live Bundle F full-stack probes are already green for the shipped in-scope slice.
  - Plain-English impact: We are keeping current Lidarr behavior unchanged for now to avoid introducing a new DB/settings migration and delete-flow behavior change in this polish slice; these deltas are now tracked in `.agents/plans/current/lidarr-quality-delete-safety-long-term-review/PLAN.md`.

## Extracted Long-Term Features (From Bundle F)

1. MBID release-correction and interactive release selection
- Plan: `plans/current/mbid-release-correction-long-term-review/MBID_RELEASE_CORRECTION_LONG_TERM_REVIEW_PLAN.md`
- Recommended follow-up slices:
  - Slice A: backend lookup contracts (`/enrichment/search/musicbrainz/*`) + tests
  - Slice B: metadata editor assisted MBID lookup integration
  - Slice C: interactive release list/grab flow

2. Lidarr quality profile + queue-aware delete safety
- Plan: `.agents/plans/current/lidarr-quality-delete-safety-long-term-review/PLAN.md`
- Recommended follow-up slices:
  - Slice A: schema/settings contract (`lidarrQualityProfileId`) + tests
  - Slice B: service runtime quality-profile wiring
  - Slice C: queue-aware delete guards + call-site semantics reconciliation

## Phase 5: Documentation Sync and Closeout (For Shipped Work Only)

### Task 5.1: Sync fork docs for delivered Bundle F deltas
- Status: Complete
- Deliverable: Accurate, scoped updates to release/fork docs after implementation tasks complete.
- References:
  - `CHANGELOG.md`
  - `README.md`
  - `docs/FORK_DIFF.md`
  - `docs/FORK_DIFF_COMMITS.md`
- Completion Summary:
  - Delivered: Synced fork docs for shipped Bundle F metadata/library polish behavior and refreshed fork-diff snapshot metadata to current branch head.

    - `docs/FORK_DIFF_COMMITS.md` (regenerated append-only commit inventory metadata and commit list from `upstream/main..HEAD`)
    - `README.md` reviewed and confirmed no additional user-facing wording changes were required for this slice.

    - `git log --oneline upstream/main..HEAD | wc -l` -> `185`
    - `git log --oneline --no-merges upstream/main..HEAD | wc -l` -> `148`
    - `git diff --shortstat upstream/main..HEAD` -> `292 files changed, 44967 insertions(+), 4755 deletions(-)`
  - Plain-English impact: Release notes and fork-delta docs now accurately describe the metadata/library reliability improvements that were actually shipped in this slice.

## Archive Status

This execution slice is functionally complete and archived.

plan.title: BUNDLE_F_METADATA_LIBRARY_POLISH_EXECUTION_PLAN.md
plan.objective:
1. Normalize upstream lookup inputs consistently where this improves external match quality without broad behavior churn.
2. Harden image proxy/cache path parity and ensure cache lifecycle is bounded.
3. Validate metadata editing, cover-art, and download/release flows against regressions.
4. Keep MBID UI and Lidarr profile deltas ready but gated until explicit promotion.
5. Sync docs only for behavior that is actually shipped.
